question:
Malik Washington: Anytime Touchdown

Description: In the upcoming NFL game, scheduled for November 30 at 1:00 PM ET:

This market will resolve to "Yes" if Malik Washington scores a touchdown at any point during regulation or overtime.

This market will resolve to "No" if Malik Washington does not score a touchdown during regulation or overtime.

Qualifying Touchdowns:

- A rushing touchdown by the player

- A receiving touchdown caught by the player

- A special teams touchdown (e.g. punt/kick return)

Passing touchdowns do not qualify. Defensive touchdowns will be credited to the defensive unit, not individually credited to the player who scores.

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed. If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50-50.

If the player is listed as inactive or otherwise does not take a snap, the market will resolve "No".

The resolution source will be the official NFL game book as published on NFL.com.

Resolution source: https://www.nfl.com/scores

evidences.content: # Malik Washington start or sit: Week 13 fantasy football advice We break down the fantasy football outlook of Devin Neal ahead of the Dolphins’ Week 13 matchup vs. the Saints. FBN-AMFOOT-ESP-NFL-DOLPHINS-COMMANDERS FBN-AMFOOT-ESP-NFL-DOLPHINS-COMMANDERS Week 13 of the 2025 NFL season rolls on this afternoon with a matchup featuring the New Orleans Saints visiting the Miami Dolphins at 1 p.m. ET on FOX. The Dolphins are fresh off their bye week and the last time we saw them, they were slipping past the Commanders for a 16-13 overtime victory in Madrid. Wide receiver Malik Washington had a hand in the win as he’s been a reliable hand for the offense in recent weeks. Below, we’ll go over Washington’s outlook in fantasy football for Week 13. [...] #### Fantasy Football analysis: Dolphins WR Malik Washington Washington made a few big grabs in the Dolphins’ victory over the Commanders two weeks ago, catching all three of his targets for 42 yards. Those numbers are in line with what the second-year wideout has put up over the past month as he’s been a decent secondary receiver option behind Jaylen Waddle. evidences.title: [Quick Guide] The Rules of Football
evidences.content: ## Basic rules of football In football, the object of the game is to score more points than the opposing team, by carrying the ball into the opposing team’s end zone or by kicking the ball through the opponent’s goalposts. In football, there are two teams of 11 players each. Even though only 11 players from each side are allowed on the field at once, a team is actually made up of 46 players for a gameday. During the season a team is allowed to have 53 players actively playing in their roster. This means 7 people will be inactive on a gameday, since a team for a gameday consists of 46 players. The team with more points at the end of the game wins. [...] #### Touchdown A touchdown is worth six points and is the most common way to score in football. A touchdown is scored when a team either carries the ball into the opposing team’s end zone or catches a pass in the end zone. The team in possession of the ball needs to move the ball forward by at least 10 yards while they are on the offense. They have 4 chances, or downs, to do so. If they manage, the count resets and now they get another four downs to push the ball another 10 yards. If the offensive team didn’t manage to move the ba...

evidences.content: Most/least statistical categories, in which one or more of the games do not take place, will be settled based on the results of the other games played in the specified time period (e.g., Most TDs scored in Sunday Games). A selection must be a clear outright winner of this market. In the event of a tie, dead-heat rules will apply. All specials markets will be settled based on the results of the league’s governing body, nfl.com A player must participate in the game and play at least one snap for bets to stand. [...] EX: Either Kyle Schwarber or Shohei Ohtani to hit a home run. If Schwarber has already hit a home run, and Ohtani is inactive in a later game, bet will settle as a win. If one player is deemed inactive before either player has participated, bet will settle as a Void. Where markets such as ‘Team to score most runs’ and ‘Highest scoring game’ are offered, which are dependent on action from each of the day’s games, 90% or more of all scheduled games must be played and go at least 8.5 innings for bets to have action, unless the winning team in the offered special took part in an official shortened game and all other games went at least 8.5 innings. evidences.title: American football rules
evidences.content: [edit")] Main article: Touchdown A touchdown is earned when a player has legal possession of the ball and the ball touches or goes over the imaginary vertical plane above the opposing team's goal line. After a touchdown, the scoring team attempts a try play for 1 or 2 points (see below). A successful touchdown is signaled by an official extending both arms vertically above the head. A touchdown is worth six points, except in the defunct WFL where it was worth seven points. For statistical purposes, the player who advances the ball into or catches it in the end zone is credited with the touchdown. If a forward pass was thrown on the play, the throwing player is credited with a passing touchdown. #### Field goal (3 points) [edit")] Main article: Field goal .jpg) Field goal attempt [...] On offense, points are scored by advancing the ball into the opponent's end zone for a touchdown worth six points or by kicking the ball from the playing field through the raised vertical posts (the goalposts) situated on the end line of the end zone for a field goal worth three points. After scoring a touchdown, the offense is given an additional opportunity to attempt to score during an un-timed...

evidences.title: What are NFL roster designations? IR, PUP, NFI explained - ESPN
evidences.content: Players on the PUP list can participate in all team activities except practice. They can be activated at any point during camp once they are medically cleared. A player can't be placed on the PUP list after they have practiced or played in a preseason game. Players on the active/PUP list can be moved to the reserve/PUP list during the preseason roster cutdown period. Players won't count against the active roster limit if they're placed on the reserve/PUP list and they must miss the first four games of the season. NFI: Non-football injury/illness [...] Players who are placed on IR before the start of the regular season can miss the entire season, unless they are designated to return when the roster is trimmed to 53 players prior to the start of the season. ## Editor's Picks ## Which NFL teams have never won a Super Bowl? ESPN ## Which NFL teams have won back-to-back Super Bowls? Alex Kennedy ## What are the longest field goals in NFL history? ESPN PUP: Physically unable to perform Like players on IR, players are put on the active/physically unable to perform list (or PUP) at the start of training camp because of a football-related injury.
